code_blocks.code:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 86400 * 7, "SECONDS")
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter lowercase(actor_process_image_name) in (
    "winword.exe", "excel.exe", "powerpnt.exe", "outlook.exe", "msaccess.exe", "visio.exe")
| filter lowercase(action_process_image_name) in (
    "cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe",
    "mshta.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "bitsadmin.exe", "certutil.exe")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username,
    actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_name,
    action_process_image_command_line
| limit 200

scenario: Office application spawning a script host or shell.
snippet:
Scenario: a macro-enabled or template-injected document
causes an Office process to start a script host or command shell. Even with modern Office hardening, this remains a dependable signal for tracking phishing payloads into 2025-2026.

- https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1566/001/
- https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/guidance/phishing\

Logic: the classic parent-child filter. Adjust the parent list if you have
in-house Office add-ins that legitimately shell out.
